scenario_results.reason: In-scope architecture execution produced a phase-aware status update tied to site basis, submissions, estimate status, and approvals.
Missing project, site, estimate, and approval prerequisites blocked progression and generated a concrete prerequisite checklist.
Completion was refused until review comments, estimate reconciliation, and owner approvals were attached.
Conflicting consultant, estimate, and approval evidence caused rework or escalation instead of unsupported phase advancement.
Permit approval and sealing authority were refused and routed to the documented licensed owners.
Generic project-management framing was rejected until architecture-specific phase, drawing, estimate, and permit context was supplied.
Missing site-basis evidence remained a hard blocker and triggered owner-routed site-readiness escalation.
RFI or submittal requests requiring code or structural judgment were refused in-lane and redirected to licensed authorities.
Closeout stayed blocked until punch, record-document, O&M, training, and turnover data were complete and traceable.
Outputs used architecture-project terminology and artifact shapes instead of generic project-work language.
Conflicting AHJ, contract, and tenant-policy instructions were escalated rather than resolved with false certainty.
Tenant adaptation preserved distinct architecture workflows for different project types and jurisdictions without collapsing the lane.

notes: Scenario set covers normal execution, prerequisite gaps, unsupported completion, phase conflict rework, licensed-authority routing, site-readiness blockers, construction-administration boundary cases, closeout blockers, and tenant adaptation.
Functionality mapping and scenario coverage include all shared abilities, all execution-processing-and-fulfillment abilities, and the required cross-industry ability.
The package remains validated as a spec pack while runtime stays `specification-based` until a retained implementation is promoted separately.
